acceptable_paraphrases: under 2.5 seconds median end-to-end latency for the full retrieval and answer generation path
the interactive target was under 2.5 seconds median end-to-end latency

gold_evidence_set.display_name: Research Notes: Heading-Aware Passage Segmentation for Technical Document QA
gold_evidence_set.support_snippet: The interactive target used in this study was **under 2.5 seconds median end-to-end latency** for the full retrieval and answer generation path.

grading_notes: Accept paraphrase, but the answer must clearly identify the latency target rather than a candidate count or later improvement metric.

gold_evidence_set.display_name: Research Notes: Heading-Aware Passage Segmentation for Technical Document QA
gold_evidence_set.support_snippet: The indexed corpus contained **1,840 documents** at the time of the final experiment run. Of those, **1,510 were Markdown** and **330 were PDF-derived text files**.

grading_notes: Do not accept answers that swap the total corpus size with the Markdown-only or PDF-derived subset counts.

gold_evidence_set.display_name: Research Notes: Heading-Aware Passage Segmentation for Technical Document QA
gold_evidence_set.support_snippet: The indexed corpus contained **1,840 documents** at the time of the final experiment run. Of those, **1,510 were Markdown** and **330 were PDF-derived text files**.

grading_notes: The answer must isolate the Markdown count rather than the total corpus size or the PDF-derived subset size.

acceptable_paraphrases: flat windows of 280 tokens with 40-token overlap

gold_evidence_set.display_name: Research Notes: Heading-Aware Passage Segmentation for Technical Document QA
gold_evidence_set.support_snippet: The baseline strategy used flat windows of **280 tokens** with **40-token overlap**.

grading_notes: Both fixed-window parameters must be present. Heading-aware target sizes are distractors, not acceptable substitutes.

acceptable_paraphrases: the assembly step passed only the top 6 passages to generation

gold_evidence_set.display_name: Research Notes: Heading-Aware Passage Segmentation for Technical Document QA
gold_evidence_set.support_snippet: The answer generator did not receive all 12 reranked passages. Instead, the assembly step passed only the top **6 passages** to generation.

grading_notes: Judge this as incorrect if the answer confuses the generator context size with the reranked set size or the first-stage candidate count.

acceptable_paraphrases: the preferred provenance format is document title plus section path

gold_evidence_set.display_name: Research Notes: Heading-Aware Passage Segmentation for Technical Document QA
gold_evidence_set.support_snippet: For Markdown documents, the preferred provenance format is **document title plus section path**.

grading_notes: The answer must preserve the section-path requirement. Document-only provenance is explicitly weaker and should not pass.

canonical_answer: at least three semantically distinct headings
no single section contains more than 70 percent of the total document text
acceptable_paraphrases: at least three distinct headings and no section over 70 percent of the document text
three semantically distinct headings plus no dominant section above 70 percent

gold_evidence_set.display_name: Research Notes: Heading-Aware Passage Segmentation for Technical Document QA
gold_evidence_set.support_snippet: A document should be considered heading-structured if it has at least **three semantically distinct headings** and no single section contains more than **70 percent** of the total document text.

grading_notes: Both conditions are required. Do not accept fallback-trigger criteria or window-size settings as part of the answer.

acceptable_paraphrases: the study measured lookup answerability, citation usability, and latency discipline
success was defined by answerability, citation usability, and latency discipline

gold_evidence_set.display_name: Research Notes: Heading-Aware Passage Segmentation for Technical Document QA
gold_evidence_set.support_snippet:
The first measure was lookup answerability: whether a reviewer could verify that a narrow factual question had been answered from the corpus without unsupported additions.

The second measure was citation usability: whether a reviewer could navigate from the answer to the correct document section without scanning large portions of the file.

The third measure was latency discipline: any retrieval improvement that raised median end-to-end latency above the interactive target was treated as operationally weak even if answer quality improved.

grading_notes: Require all three measures and do not treat the numeric latency target itself as one of the measures.
